2605.07924.title: Trajectory as the Teacher: Few-Step Discrete Flow Matching via Energy-Navigated Distillation
2605.07924.paper_summary: Discrete flow matching generates text by iteratively transforming noise tokens into coherent language, but may require hundreds of forward passes. Distillation uses the multi-step trajectory to train a student to reproduce the process in a few steps. When the student underperforms, the usual explanation is insufficient capacity. We argue the opposite: the trajectory is the bottleneck, not the student. Each training trajectory is built through a chain of blind stochastic jumps with no evaluation of sequence quality; a single bad decision at an early midpoint propagates through subsequent steps, yet the student must imitate the result. Trajectory-Shaped Discrete Flow Matching (TS-DFM) replaces these blind jumps with guided navigation: a lightweight energy compass evaluates candidate continuations at each midpoint, selecting the most coherent. All shaping is training-only; inference cost is unchanged. On 170M-parameter language modeling, the shaped student at 8 steps achieves 32% lower perplexity than the 1,024-step teacher while being 128x faster, with gains consistent across source distributions and three evaluators of increasing scale. TS-DFM achieves the best perplexity of any discrete-generation baseline we compare against, including methods trained on 6x more data or using 5x larger models.
2605.07924.paper_summary_zh: 離散流匹配通過迭代地將噪聲標記轉換為連貫的語言來生成文本，但可能需要數百次前向傳遞。蒸餾利用多步軌跡來訓練學生在幾步內重現該過程。當學生表現不佳時，通常的解釋是容量不足。我們的觀點正好相反：軌跡是瓶頸，而不是學生。每個訓練軌跡都是通過一系列盲目的隨機跳躍構建的，沒有對序列質量進行評估；在早期中點的單一錯誤決策會在隨後的步驟中傳播，但學生必須模仿結果。軌跡形狀離散流匹配（TS-DFM）用引導導航取代這些盲跳：一個輕量級的能量羅盤在每個中點評估候選延續，選擇最連貫的。所有形狀都是僅限於訓練的；推理成本不變。在170M參數的語言建模中，經過形狀處理的學生在8步內實現了比1,024步教師低32%的困惑度，同時速度快128倍，並且在各種來源分佈和三個逐步增大的評估者中，增益是一致的。TS-DFM在我們比較的任何離散生成基準中實現了最佳困惑度，包括在6倍更多數據上訓練或使用5倍更大模型的方法。
2605.07924.author: Amin Karimi Monsefi et.al.
2605.07924.authors: Amin Karimi Monsefi, Dominic Culver, Nikhil Bhendawade, Manuel R. Ciosici, Yizhe Zhang, Irina Belousova
